input.background: Reconsider the conditions of the illustrative example that compared cholesterol in the two groups.
input.question: What is the power of a test of $H_0: \mu_1=\mu_2$ when $\alpha=0.05$ (two-sided), $n=30$ per group, $\mu_1-\mu_2=0.25$, and $\sigma^2=0.25$?

group_mean_difference.description: True difference between the population means of the two groups (mu1 - mu2).

population_variance.description: Common population variance of the outcome in each group (sigma^2).

group_sample_size.description: Sample size per group in the two-sample comparison.

significance_level.description: Two-sided Type I error rate alpha for the hypothesis test.

test_power.description: Power of the two-sided test of H0: mu1 = mu2 under the specified alternative and parameters.

answer.value: Use the specified mean difference, variance, sample size, and significance level to compute the noncentrality parameter for a two-sample z or t test of equality of means, then obtain the power as the probability of rejecting H0 under the alternative distribution.
answer.description: Text description of the analysis process to compute the test power.
